home.title: Live to fight. Fight to live

accessRules.anonymous: Anonymous players can only play with bot or bot vs bot. Login is required for online mode.
accessRules.registered: Just one click to play online. Your profile, stats, and progression will be saved for future battles.

home.lore1: From this command center, every warship moves under the same pulse of orders: radar sweeps the sea again, formations are corrected, and a tactical scenario can harden into a live battlespace on a single short command.
home.lore2: Yet every coordinate on the naval chart still carries the echo of old ambushes, where a captain's composure is tested between alarm sirens and radar haze; on open water at night, only a precise order can lead to the decisive shot.
home.commandDeckDesc: Select a scenario, adjust system settings, or authenticate to unlock online operations.

gameHistory.label: Online matches
gameHistory.empty: No finished online matches yet. Win your first battle at sea.
gameHistory.loadError: Could not load match history. Try again later.

leaderboard.title: Commander leaderboard
leaderboard.subtitle: Ranked by competitive ELO from online PvP. Higher is stronger.

rank.eloRulesIntro: After every online PvP match, both players’ ELO is updated. Points are not fixed (+/- the same every time): they depend on win or loss and on how strong your opponent was compared to you.
rank.eloRulePlain1: If you are much stronger than your opponent and you win, you gain only a small amount (the system treats that outcome as likely).
rank.eloRulePlain2: If you are much stronger but you lose, you lose a lot of rating (a big upset against you).
rank.eloRulePlain3: If you beat someone stronger or evenly matched, you usually gain more — a reward for a harder win.
rank.eloRulesKTitle: K-factor — how big each swing is
rank.eloRulesKIntro: New players and top players do not move at the same speed from one match:
rank.eloRuleKLow: Below 1000 ELO: K = 40 — ratings move faster (friendly for beginners).
rank.eloRuleKMid: From 1000 through 1599 ELO: K = 25 — moderate, steady changes.
rank.eloRuleKHigh: 1600 ELO and above: K = 15 — slower changes; climbing needs consistent wins.
rank.eloRulesFormulaNote: Technical detail: we use the standard Elo model — an expected win chance from the rating gap (400-point scale), then new rating = old rating + K × (actual result 1 win / 0 loss minus that expectation).

apprenticeSailor.name: Apprentice Sailor
apprenticeSailor.blurb: Starting tier for every new account.

combatNavigator.blurb: Solid grasp of the core rules.

oceanConqueror.name: Ocean Conqueror

features.ai: Practice mode against adaptive bot difficulty.

support.label: Support the team
support.title: If you want to back the project
support.description: Your support helps the team keep polishing the experience, expanding the gameplay, and shipping stronger builds.

slides.title: {{gameName}} brings the player into a sci-fi naval command room from the very first screen.
slides.description: The overall interface, presentation style, and match atmosphere come first before the showcase moves into the detailed gameplay flow.
slides.ctaHint: Scroll down to continue the showcase

panels.title: Hero Overview
panels.description: The main game interface appears immediately, placing the player inside a modern naval duel with a clear command-deck feel.
panels.frameHint: Drop in your strongest opening composition here.

panels.title: World & Tone
panels.description: The board layout, tactical HUD, and sci-fi atmosphere define the environment the player interacts with throughout the match.
panels.frameHint: Use a close-up that sells the universe and style.

slides.title: Gameplay revolves around reading the board, choosing targets, and reviewing the result of each match.
slides.description: The flow moves from reading the current match state, to making a turn-based firing decision, and finally to the result summary with win-loss information and hit probability.

panels.title: Deploy The Fleet
panels.description: The player reads the state of both sides and prepares to enter the first firing turn.
panels.frameHint: Use a setup or opening battle state capture here.

panels.title: Choose The Shot
panels.description: Here the player reads the information already revealed on the enemy board, picks a target cell, and commits to the next turn-based shot.
panels.frameHint: Pick a screenshot that communicates the moment before impact.

panels.title: Match Result Screen
panels.description: The post-match summary presents the win-loss outcome along with the player's hit probability.
panels.frameHint: Use a frame that highlights hit and miss clarity.

slides.title: Before the match starts, players move through room setup, lobby review, and match entry in one connected flow.
slides.description: The pre-match experience covers room setup, settings review, real-time chat, and the transition into the game.

panels.title: Room Settings
panels.description: The host configures the room settings for the match before both players move into the waiting state and start the game.
panels.frameHint: A fleet or board setup capture fits well here.

panels.title: Pre-Match Lobby
panels.description: The two-player waiting room lets both players review the host's settings and chat with each other in real time before starting.
panels.frameHint: Use a shot of the placement phase with ship silhouettes visible.

panels.title: Find Or Join A Room
panels.description: The online flow moves from creating or joining a room, through synchronization, into the match itself.
panels.frameHint: Choose a waiting room or room list screenshot later.

slides.title: The bot reads board state to search for targets, follow up after a hit, and maintain pressure until the ship is finished.
slides.description: Its behavior is split into three clear phases: probing open cells, tracking after a confirmed hit, and pressing the attack until the target is sunk.

panels.title: Search Mode
panels.description: The bot starts by probing open cells and narrowing the area where a ship is likely to be.
panels.frameHint: A clean board state with early bot actions works well here.

panels.title: Reaction After A Hit
panels.description: After a hit, the bot checks neighboring cells to determine the ship's direction.
panels.frameHint: Use a frame where the bot has clearly locked onto a ship.

panels.title: Pressure And Finish
panels.description: Once it has enough information, the bot keeps attacking until the ship is sunk.
panels.frameHint: A later-match board state is ideal for this panel.

slides.title: The final slide highlights the team behind the project and everyone who helped support it.
slides.description: Members, GitHub links, and support details close the showcase in a clear and complete way.

header.deploymentSectorConfigured: Deployment sector configured for a {{rows}} x {{cols}} grid
header.systemLogWaiting: System log: waiting for fleet confirmation

aiDifficulty.label: AI DIFFICULTY
aiDifficulty.guideRandom: Random: AI places ships and chooses shots with simple random logic.
aiDifficulty.guideProbability: Probability: AI estimates likely ship positions from remaining cells and hunts highest-probability targets.
aiDifficulty.guideLlm: LLM: AI calls the server-side GenAI model to suggest the next shot, with safe fallback when the provider is unavailable.

step1.configureGridHint: Configure the tactical grid before finalizing fleet composition.
step1.totalCellsInfo: Total cells occupied: {{used}} / {{max}} max (50% of board)
step1.noShips: No ships defined. Add at least one.
step1.invalidFleetError: Ships take up too many cells. Reduce sizes/counts or increase the board.

placement.guideStep1: Choose a ship instance from the list and place it on the grid.
placement.guideStep2: Use Random, Rotate, and Clear controls to speed up placement adjustments.
placement.guideStep3: When every requirement in the checklist is complete, press Ready.
placement.cannotPlaceHere: Cannot place here: out of board or colliding with another ship.
placement.allShipsPlaced: All ships placed. You are ready to start.
placement.placementInstructions: Select a ship instance, then click a board cell to place it.
placement.randomPlacementFailed: Random placement failed. Try increasing board size or reducing fleet density.
placement.hullDeployHint: Select a hull and deploy it on the tactical grid.

header.title: Command Deck
header.description: Two active boards, a 30 second turn clock, and live fleet tracking. Red cells are confirmed hits, white cells are missed shots.
header.playerTurnHint: Choose a target on the enemy board before the timer runs out.
header.botTurnHint: The bot is selecting its next shot.
header.finished: Battle complete. You can redeploy or go back to setup.

status.ready: Battle stations set. Open with your first shot.
status.playerTimeout: Your turn expired. The bot takes over.
status.playerWins: Final hit landed at {{cell}}. Enemy fleet destroyed.
status.botWins: Bot's strike at {{cell}} finished your fleet.

missingState.title: No battle setup found

errors.TIMEOUT: Request timed out. Please try again.
errors.NETWORK_ERROR: Network error. Please check your internet connection.
errors.spriteContextUnavailable: Could not create a 2D canvas context for sprite extraction.
errors.spriteDirectionCountInvalid: Expected 5 ship sprites for direction {{direction}}, but found {{count}}.
errors.INVALID_EMAIL: Please enter a valid email address.
errors.USERNAME_TOO_LONG: Username must be at most 20 characters.
errors.USERNAME_INVALID_FORMAT: Username can only contain letters, numbers, and spaces.
errors.PASSWORD_TOO_SHORT: Password must be at least 8 characters.
errors.PASSWORD_TOO_LONG: Password must be at most 72 characters.
errors.NEW_PASSWORD_SAME_AS_OLD: New password must be different from your current password.
errors.SIGNATURE_TOO_LONG: Signature must be at most 200 characters.
errors.SIGNATURE_INVALID_FORMAT: Signature must not contain HTML tags.
errors.FORUM_FORBIDDEN: You can only edit your own post or comment.
errors.FORUM_POST_TITLE_TOO_LONG: Post title must be at most 150 characters.
errors.FORUM_POST_CONTENT_TOO_LONG: Post content must be at most 5000 characters.
errors.FORUM_COMMENT_TOO_LONG: Comment content must be at most 1200 characters.
errors.FORUM_VOTE_INVALID: Vote must be either upvote or downvote.
errors.ADMIN_CANNOT_BAN_SELF: You cannot ban your own account.
errors.BAN_DAYS_REQUIRED: Temporary bans require a valid days value.
errors.UNKNOWN_ERROR: Something went wrong. Please try again.

gameBattle.chatPlaceholder: No messages yet. Open the channel and send the first ping.
gameBattle.heatMapExplainUnsupported: The current attacker is not using probability mode, so there is no heat-map score formula to explain.
gameBattle.heatMapPlacementSummary: Base heat counts distinct legal ship placements that still pass through this cell: {{count}} total (each adds +1).
gameBattle.heatMapSourcePlacements: Valid ship placements crossing this cell
gameBattle.heatMapSourceHits: Hit cells directly touching this cell
gameBattle.heatMapSourceClusters: Damaged clusters that make this cell an open target
gameBattle.heatMapParitySourceActive: {{cell}} is on the preferred parity pattern, so it gets the bonus
gameBattle.heatMapParitySourceInactive: {{cell}} is off the preferred parity pattern, so this bonus is 0
gameBattle.heatMapCenterSource: This depends on {{cell}} itself: the closer it is to the board center, the larger this bonus becomes
gameBattle.heatMapTheoryTermBaseDesc: The number of still-valid placements for remaining ships that pass through the current cell. This is the core probability signal.
gameBattle.heatMapTheoryTermParityDesc: A checkerboard-style pattern. The bot prefers this rhythm while searching because it covers the board more efficiently.
gameBattle.heatMapTheoryTermCenterDesc: A small tie-break bonus for cells closer to the middle of the board.
gameBattle.heatMapTheoryTermAdjacencyDesc: Extra score when the current cell touches a confirmed hit. If part of a ship was found, its neighbours become more suspicious.
gameBattle.heatMapTheoryTermClusterDesc: A group of connected hits that has not been sunk yet. When several clusters exist, the bot must decide which one to pursue first.
gameBattle.heatMapTheoryHowLine1: Step 1: the bot ignores all cells that were already fired on and only evaluates legal remaining cells.
gameBattle.heatMapTheoryHowLine2: Step 2: if there are unsunk hits, the bot switches from broad search mode into pursuit mode around that damaged cluster.
gameBattle.heatMapTheoryHowLine3: Step 3: for each candidate cell, the bot adds multiple score sources together. Each source answers: why is this cell suspicious?
gameBattle.heatMapTheoryHowLine4: Step 4: the cell with the highest final score becomes the shot choice. The later A, B, C... pages show the per-cell details column by column.

result.victoryDesc: All enemy ships have been sunk. The seas are ours.
result.defeatDesc: Your fleet has been destroyed. Retreat and regroup.
